name: Awakening: Body
text:
The man's hand is around your throat.

This isn't sparring. This isn't practice. This is the alley behind the market where nobody comes after dark, and his fingers are closing, and the world is going narrow and bright at the edges.

You can feel your heartbeat in your temples, your wrists, the backs of your knees. Each pulse is louder than the last — not faster, louder, like your blood has decided it will not be stopped by something as simple as a hand.

Then something shifts. Not outside — inside. A heat that isn't heat. A pressure from the core of you, pushing outward through muscle and bone and skin. Your body doesn't ask your permission. It simply decides: no.

Your hand comes up and closes around his wrist. You squeeze. The bones in his forearm grind together and his grip breaks like a rotten branch.

He stumbles back. You're standing in the alley, breathing hard, and your skin is humming. Not trembling — humming. Like a struck bell that hasn't stopped ringing.

You look at your hand. It looks the same. But something behind it is different — a steadiness that wasn't there before, as if the shaking you've always carried has finally stopped.

The man runs. You let him. You stand in the alley for a long time, breathing, feeling the hum settle into something quieter. Not gone. Just yours now.
